findings.title: Unhealthy window check is inverted
findings.impact: The tracker treats windows with a success rate greater than the threshold as unhealthy, so a healthy URMA data plane can trigger failover while persistently failing windows below the threshold are ignored. This breaks the core failover behavior and can leave clients pinned to a bad worker.
findings.fix: Change the predicate to return true only when successCount is strictly less than totalCount * successRateRatio.
